title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · Repository Trace: Gita Fsmonitor Untracked Cache Index
summary: A busy monorepo accumulates packs, stale worktrees, slow reachability walks, background jobs, and backup gaps while developers and CI operate concurrently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita fsmonitor untracked cache index increment in measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate built-in fsmonitor, untracked cache, split index, sparse index, preload index, and filesystem support using correctness-first benchmarks.
Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Fsmonitor Untracked Cache Index
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G14 is a theory in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Fsmonitor Untracked Cache Index contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G14 is a worked example in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. A defensible solution applies Gita Fsmonitor Untracked Cache Index, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 1
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning
git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack
# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Fsmonitor Untracked Cache Index evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-fsmonitor-untracked-cache-index-1ea4ms" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Fsmonitor Untracked Cache Index into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-fsmonitor-untracked-cache-index-1ea4ms
# scenario-key: trace-1ea4ms probe-sm4ae1 boundary-a4ms1e owner-1ea4msx fault-1ea4msz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1e candidate=b00b1ms
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning
git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack
# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11ce1e candidate=c0ffees
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need
